CLAUDE.md - selmaR Package

Source: CLAUDE.md

Git Commit Rules

  • NEVER add Co-Authored-By lines referencing Claude, Anthropic, or any AI tool
  • NEVER mention Claude, AI, or any AI tool in commit messages
  • NEVER add Claude or any AI tool as a contributor in DESCRIPTION, README, or anywhere else
  • The user pays for the license, drives the design, and owns all output — credit belongs to the user alone

Mission

Build selmaR — an R package that wraps the SELMA student management system API. This is a resaleable, general-purpose package for any SELMA customer, not just MissionReady HQ. It should follow the same patterns as packages like activecampaignr — clean, documented, testable.

SELMA is a New Zealand student management system used by PTEs (Private Training Establishments) and TEOs (Tertiary Education Organisations). It exposes a REST API with Hydra JSON-LD pagination.

What Already Exists

The reference_code/ directory contains working R code extracted from a production analytics pipeline (MRHQ project). This code works but is tightly coupled to one organisation’s workflow. Your job is to extract the general-purpose SELMA API functionality into a proper R package.

OpenAPI Specs (canonical location)

File API Version Format Notes
data-raw/openapi/selma_v2.json v2.2.0 (OpenAPI 3.0.0) Pure JSON Paths under /app/, uses hydra:member
data-raw/openapi/selma_v3.json v3.28 (OpenAPI 3.1.0) Pure JSON Paths under /api/, uses member

Also present (do not use directly): - selma_openapi.json — root-level copy of v2 spec (original location, superseded) - selma_api_docs/selma-swagger.json — v3 spec wrapped in an HTML <script> tag (not pure JSON) - selma_api_docs/docs.json — v3 spec, pure JSON (original location, superseded by data-raw/openapi/selma_v3.json) - selma_api_docs/api_summary.md — human-readable summary of v3 endpoints - selma_api_docs/parse_swagger.py — Python script used to generate the summary

The data-raw/openapi/ specs are the build-time source of truth for schema validation. Use data-raw/build_schema_registry.R to extract field lists into R/sysdata.rda.

Reference Code Files

Directory File What to extract
reference_code/02_api_functions.R SELMA API functions: auth, pagination, data fetching, caching Core of the package — authentication, paginated fetching, Hydra JSON-LD handling, all 4 entity sync functions
reference_code/00_config.R SELMA config constants, status codes, date parsing Status code constants, date parsing helpers
reference_code/11_matching_core.R Phone normalization, programme matching, intake normalization Phone standardization (NZ/AU), URL builders — these are useful general utilities
reference_code/12_reconciliation.R Reconciliation engine (fact tables, classification) Do NOT include — this is application-specific logic, not SELMA API
reference_code/13_temporal.R SQLite snapshot management Do NOT include — application-specific
reference_code/07_selma_reconciliation.R Intake mapping, matching reports Do NOT include — application-specific
reference_code/10_EFTS_reporting.R EFTS funding calculations Consider including as a vignette/utility — EFTS pro-rata calculation is useful for any NZ TEO
reference_config/programme_type_config.csv Programme type mappings Reference only — not for the package
reference_config/selma_intake_mapping.csv Intake mapping reference Reference only
reference_tests/test_selma_api.R API connectivity tests Adapt into proper testthat tests

Package Scope

In Scope (Core)

  1. Authentication — selma_auth(email, password, base_url) → bearer token
  2. Paginated data fetching — generic selma_get(endpoint, ...) that handles Hydra JSON-LD pagination
  3. Entity-specific fetch functions:
    • selma_students(token, ...) — fetch student records
    • selma_enrolments(token, ...) — fetch enrolment records
    • selma_intakes(token, ...) — fetch intake definitions
    • selma_components(token, ...) — fetch enrolment components
    • selma_programmes(token, ...) — fetch programme definitions
    • selma_intake_enrolments(token, intake_id) — fetch nested intake+enrolment+component data
  4. Data standardization — clean Hydra JSON-LD quirks (@id URIs, id_2 renaming, clean_names)
  5. Caching layer — optional RDS caching with configurable TTL
  6. Status code constants — enrolment statuses, component statuses as package constants
  7. Date parsing — parse_selma_date() for SELMA’s ISO date strings
  8. URL builders — selma_student_url(id, base_url), selma_enrolment_url(id, base_url)

In Scope (Utilities)

  1. Phone normalization — standardize_phone() / standardize_phones() for NZ (+64) and AU (+61) formats
  2. EFTS calculation — calculate_efts() pro-rata EFTS across calendar months (useful for any NZ TEO)

Out of Scope

  • ActiveCampaign integration (that’s a separate package)
  • Reconciliation logic (application-specific)
  • Google Sheets reporting
  • Progression queue management
  • SQLite snapshot/temporal tracking
  • Contact-student bridge matching (application-specific)

Architecture Guidelines

Package Structure

selmaR/
├── DESCRIPTION
├── NAMESPACE
├── R/
│   ├── auth.R              # selma_auth(), selma_connect(), token management
│   ├── api.R               # selma_get(), pagination, Hydra handling, standardize_selma_data()
│   ├── api_version.R       # .api_config registry — version-specific path/key/auth config
│   ├── students.R          # selma_students()
│   ├── enrolments.R        # selma_enrolments()
│   ├── intakes.R           # selma_intakes()
│   ├── components.R        # selma_components()
│   ├── programmes.R        # selma_programmes()
│   ├── intake_enrolments.R # selma_intake_enrolments() — nested JSON
│   ├── cache.R             # RDS caching layer
│   ├── constants.R         # Status codes, funded statuses
│   ├── utils.R             # Date parsing, phone normalization, URL builders
│   ├── efts.R              # EFTS pro-rata calculation
│   ├── sysdata.rda         # Internal: schema registry generated by data-raw/build_schema_registry.R
│   └── selmaR-package.R    # Package-level documentation
├── man/                    # roxygen2-generated docs
├── data-raw/
│   ├── openapi/
│   │   ├── selma_v2.json   # OpenAPI 3.0.0 v2.2.0 spec (paths: /app/)
│   │   └── selma_v3.json   # OpenAPI 3.1.0 v3.28 spec (paths: /api/)
│   └── build_schema_registry.R  # Parses specs → sysdata.rda (.selma_schemas)
├── tests/
│   └── testthat/
│       ├── test-auth.R
│       ├── test-api.R
│       ├── test-students.R
│       ├── test-cache.R
│       ├── test-utils.R
│       └── test-efts.R
├── vignettes/
│   ├── getting-started.Rmd
│   └── api-v3-migration.Rmd   # Field name changes from v2 → v3
├── inst/
│   └── extdata/            # Example data for tests/vignettes
└── reference_*             # Existing code for reference (not part of package)

Design Principles

  1. Connection object pattern — selma_connect() returns a selma_connection S3 object storing base_url, token, and api_version. Passed to all fetch functions. Similar to DBI connections:

    con <- selma_connect(base_url = "https://myorg.selma.co.nz/",
                     email = "api@selma.co.nz",
                     password = "secret")   # api_version auto-detected
students <- selma_students(con)

  2. API version in connection — api_version ("v2" or "v3") lives on the connection object, not as a per-request parameter. A SELMA instance runs one version. Default is NULL (auto-detect by probing the API at connect time). Version-specific config (path prefix, member key, auth endpoint) lives in .api_config in R/api_version.R.

  3. Version config registry — All version-specific constants are in .api_config, never hardcoded inline:

    .api_config <- list(
  v2 = list(path_prefix = "app/", member_key = "hydra:member", total_key = "hydra:totalItems", ...),
  v3 = list(path_prefix = "api/", member_key = "member",       total_key = "totalItems",      ...)
)

  4. Schema-driven field handling — R/sysdata.rda contains .selma_schemas, a nested list of expected fields and ID column names per entity per API version, generated at build time from data-raw/openapi/. The standardize_selma_data() function uses this instead of a hardcoded switch(). Soft validation: warn (do not error) when expected fields are missing, so SELMA API changes produce actionable messages, not silent wrong data.

  5. Fail-loud member extraction — Never do page_data[["hydra:member"]] inline. Always use extract_members(page_data, api_version) which: tries the version-appropriate key, falls back to the other version’s key with a warning, and errors immediately (with response keys listed) if neither is found.

  6. No field translation layer — v3 returns different field names than v2 (e.g. first_name vs forename). The package surfaces what the API returns. Breaking field name changes are documented in vignettes/api-v3-migration.Rmd, not papered over with renaming logic.

  7. Environment variable auth — Support SELMA_BASE_URL, SELMA_EMAIL, SELMA_PASSWORD env vars as defaults (like most R API packages).

  8. Tidy output — All functions return tibbles with clean_names() (snake_case). No Hydra JSON-LD artifacts in user-facing output.

  9. Optional caching — Functions accept cache = TRUE/FALSE and cache_dir parameters. Cache uses RDS files with configurable TTL. Caching is opt-in, not default.

  10. Progress reporting — Support progressr for long-running fetches (thousands of pages). Use p = NULL default pattern for backward compatibility.

  11. No hardcoded org details — base_url, credentials, and org-specific mappings must be parameters, never constants.

  12. Minimal dependencies — httr2, jsonlite, tibble, janitor, cli, rlang. Suggest (not require) progressr. Do NOT add rapiclient — it does not handle Hydra pagination and pins httr as a dependency.

SELMA API Details

API Versions

v2 (legacy) v3 (current)
OpenAPI spec data-raw/openapi/selma_v2.json data-raw/openapi/selma_v3.json
OpenAPI version 3.0.0 3.1.0
SELMA version 2.2.0 3.28
Path prefix /app/ /api/
Member key hydra:member member
Total items key hydra:totalItems totalItems
Field casing Inconsistent (camelCase/PascalCase) snake_case
Example: student name forename, surname first_name, last_name

Authentication

  • v2: POST to {base_url}api/login_check with _username and _password form fields
  • v3: POST to {base_url}api/auth (check spec for exact field names)
  • Returns JSON with token field (JWT bearer token)
  • Token expires — handle re-auth gracefully

Pagination (Hydra JSON-LD)

  • GET {base_url}{path_prefix}{endpoint}?page=1&itemsPerPage=30
  • Response contains a member array (hydra:member in v2, member in v3) and pagination view
  • Keep fetching while next link exists in the view
  • Each member may have @id field (Hydra URI) — strip this in standardization
  • Always use extract_members(page_data, api_version) — never access the member key inline

Data Quirks

  • Some endpoints return id_2 instead of id when the entity already has an id field — rename to id
  • v2: Field names are inconsistent (camelCase, PascalCase, lowercase) — clean_names() normalises them
  • v3: Fields are already snake_case from the API, but clean_names() is still applied for consistency
  • Nullable fields may be NULL, NA, or empty string — normalize to NA
  • All dates are ISO 8601 strings — parse to Date objects
  • ID columns should be character (not numeric) for safe joining — some are returned as integers

Endpoints Reference

See data-raw/openapi/selma_v2.json and data-raw/openapi/selma_v3.json for full field documentation. Also see selma_api_docs/api_summary.md for a human-readable v3 endpoint summary.

Key endpoints (v2 path → v3 path): - /app/students → /api/students — student contact details - /app/enrolments → /api/enrolments — enrolment records - /app/intakes → /api/intakes — intake/cohort definitions - /app/enrolment_components → /api/enrolment_components — component-level data - /app/programmes → /api/programmes — programme definitions - /app/intake_enrolments?intakeid=N → check v3 spec — nested intake+enrolment+component data (special handling)

Status Codes (Constants to Export)

# Enrolment statuses
SELMA_STATUS_CONFIRMED <- "C"
SELMA_STATUS_COMPLETED <- "FC"
SELMA_STATUS_INCOMPLETE <- "FI"
SELMA_STATUS_WITHDRAWN <- "WR"
SELMA_STATUS_WITHDRAWN_SDR <- "WS"
SELMA_STATUS_EARLY_WITHDRAWN <- "ER"
SELMA_STATUS_DEFERRED <- "D"
SELMA_STATUS_CANCELLED <- "X"
SELMA_STATUS_PENDING <- "P"

# Revenue-generating statuses (for filtering)
SELMA_FUNDED_STATUSES <- c("C", "FC", "FI")
SELMA_ALL_FUNDED_STATUSES <- c("C", "FC", "FI", "WR", "WS")  # includes withdrawn (still funded)

# Funding sources
SELMA_FUNDING_GOVT <- "01"         # Government Funded
SELMA_FUNDING_INTL <- "02"         # International Fee-Paying
SELMA_FUNDING_MPTT <- "29"         # MPTT Level 3 and 4
SELMA_FUNDING_YG   <- "31"         # Youth Guarantee
SELMA_FUNDING_DQ37 <- "37"         # Non-degree L3-7 NZQCF

Testing Strategy

  • Unit tests: Mock HTTP responses (use httptest2 or webmockr) for pagination, auth, data cleaning
  • Integration tests: Skip on CRAN, run against live API with SELMA_TEST_BASE_URL env var
  • Reference tests: Port relevant tests from reference_tests/test_selma_api.R
  • EFTS tests: Known inputs → expected outputs for pro-rata calculation

Dependencies

Imports

  • httr2 (preferred over httr for new packages) OR httr (if simpler)
  • jsonlite
  • tibble
  • janitor (for clean_names)
  • cli (for user-facing messages)
  • rlang (for error handling)

Suggests

  • progressr (optional progress reporting)
  • readr (for cache file I/O)
  • testthat (>= 3.0.0)
  • httptest2 (for mocking)
  • knitr, rmarkdown (for vignettes)

Implementation Order

The package already has a working v2 implementation. The next phase addresses brittleness and v3 support:

  1. Phase 8 — Version config registry: Create R/api_version.R with .api_config (path prefix, member key, auth endpoint, total key per version)
  2. Phase 9 — Schema registry: Write data-raw/build_schema_registry.R to parse both OpenAPI specs into .selma_schemas in R/sysdata.rda
  3. Phase 10 — Harden R/auth.R: Add api_version param to selma_connect(), store in connection object, implement auto-detection
  4. Phase 11 — Harden R/api.R: Replace hardcoded "app/" with cfg$path_prefix, replace inline member key access with extract_members(), replace switch() ID column list with schema-driven lookup, add soft field validation warnings
  5. Phase 12 — Entity functions: Update query parameter names for v3 where they differ from v2
  6. Phase 13 — Tests: Add mocked responses for both API versions
  7. Phase 14 — Migration vignette: vignettes/api-v3-migration.Rmd documenting v2→v3 field name changes

Prior phases (completed): - Phase 1–7: Auth, pagination, entity functions, caching, utilities, EFTS, docs

Style Guide

  • Use roxygen2 for all documentation
  • Follow tidyverse style guide
  • Pipe-friendly function signatures (data as first arg where sensible)
  • All exported functions prefixed with selma_ (except utilities like standardize_phone)
  • Use cli for user-facing messages (not message() or cat())
  • Error messages should be informative — include the endpoint URL and HTTP status code on failure

On this page
